name: San Martín de la Vega and Chinchón Loop
name_original: San Martín de la Vega, Chinchón, Urtajo – ruta circular
description: This moderate 41.5-mile road cycling route near Madrid offers scenic views, historical charm, and passes through Chinchón.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around San Martín De La Vega traverse a diverse landscape shaped by the Jarama River, featuring distinctive fluvial terraces, gypsum crests, and cliffs. The region offers predominantly paved surfaces, including asphalt and concrete, ensuring smooth rides. Cyclists will encounter varied terrain, from flat riverbanks to gently undulating dry farmlands and more rugged slopes with moderate elevation gains. This area, part of the Southeast Regional Park of Madrid, provides a scenic backdrop for road cycling. Best road cycling routes around San Martín De La Vega The most popular road cycling route is Chinchón Loop from San Martín de la Vega, a 33.0 miles (53.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 23 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers varied terrain and scenic views. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is San Martín de la Vega and Chinchón Loop, a moderate 41.5 miles (66.9 km) path. This longer route features significant elevation changes and expansive views of the surrounding territory. Local road cyclists also love the Shortcut via the M313 – Plaza Mayor de Chinchón loop from San Martín de la Vega, a 39.6 miles (63.7 km) trail leading through diverse landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 44 minutes. Road cycling around San Martín De La Vega is defined by its riverbanks, holm oak groves, and gypsum crests, offering a mix of flat sections and moderate climbs. The network provides options for various ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation. The routes in San Martín De La Vega are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from 6 reviews. More than 500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore San Martín De La Vega's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in San Martín De La Vega?
A: There are over 10 road cycling routes available in San Martín De La Vega, catering to various fitness levels and preferences. You'll find a good mix of easy and moderate rides.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in San Martín De La Vega?
A: The region offers diverse terrain, from the flat, high-biodiversity riverbanks of the Jarama to gently undulating dry farmlands and more rugged slopes with moderate elevation gains. Most routes feature predominantly paved surfaces, including asphalt and concrete, ensuring a smooth ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in San Martín De La Vega?
A: San Martín de la Vega generally offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures, while summer can be warm, making early morning or late afternoon rides ideal. Winter rides are also possible, though cooler.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: Yes, San Martín De La Vega has several easy road cycling routes. For instance, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38154459" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Marañosa Climb – ⛰️ END La Marañosa loop from Ensanche&lt;/a&gt; is an easy option, covering about 32 km with moderate elevation, perfect for a relaxed outing.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation in San Martín De La Vega?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking more challenging rides with significant elevation, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19944511" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Marañosa Climb – M-506 Road to Ciempozuelos loop from San Martín de la Vega&lt;/a&gt; offer substantial climbs and descents, providing a good workout and expansive views.

Q: What natural features or attractions can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: The routes traverse the diverse landscapes of the Southeast Regional Park of Madrid. You can encounter distinctive fluvial terraces, gypsum crests, and cliffs. Notable natural attractions nearby include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5123913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jarama Cliffs&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4232941" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rivas Cliffs&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features holm oak groves and pine forests, offering beautiful scenery.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots on the routes?
A: Many routes offer scenic views, particularly from the more rugged slopes and gypsum cliffs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2864570" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the confluence of the Jarama and Manzanares rivers from the Cortados de Rivas&lt;/a&gt; is a notable viewpoint. The varied topography, including riverbanks and dry farmlands, provides high chromatic contrasts and excellent scenic quality throughout your ride.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in San Martín De La Vega?
A: While many road cycling routes are designed for experienced riders, the region's dedicated cycleways and quiet roads can offer suitable sections for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977180409" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chinchón Loop from San Martín de la Vega&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that can be adapted, and nearby attractions like Lagunas Las Madres offer a peaceful natural retreat ideal for family activities.

Q: Is parking available near the road cycling routes in San Martín De La Vega?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in and around San Martín De La Vega, especially in the town center and near popular starting points for routes. It's advisable to check specific starting locations for the most convenient parking options.

Q: How accessible are the road cycling routes by public transport?
A: San Martín de la Vega is accessible by public transport from Madrid, making it possible to reach the starting points of many routes without a car. The Madrid–San Martín de la Vega Bike Path also offers a direct connection for cyclists coming from the capital.

Q: Are there loop road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in San Martín De La Vega are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977181077" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Martín de la Vega and Chinchón Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38199554" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Shortcut via the M313 – Plaza Mayor de Chinchón loop from San Martín de la Vega&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in San Martín De La Vega?
A: The road cycling routes around San Martín De La Vega are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from 6 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, well-maintained paved surfaces, and the beautiful, diverse natural landscapes that provide a scenic backdrop for their rides.

Q: Can I find routes that connect to other towns or areas?
A: Yes, several routes connect San Martín de la Vega to nearby towns and areas. The Madrid–San Martín de la Vega Bike Path offers a connection to Madrid, and routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977180409" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chinchón Loop from San Martín de la Vega&lt;/a&gt; extend to the charming town of Chinchón, allowing you to explore beyond the immediate vicinity.
tips: In San Martín de la Vega its archaeological zones stand out. The archaeological remains found in the lands of San Martín de la Vega demonstrate the human presence since prehistoric, Roman, Visigothic and Arab times.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the San Martín de la Vega, Chinchón, Urtajo circular route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate for road cycling. While it's not overly strenuous, it does require a reasonable level of physical conditioning. You can expect gentle climbs followed by exhilarating descents.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty rating and a distance of nearly 67 km, this route is generally not recommended for absolute beginners. It requires a reasonable level of fitness and experience with road cycling.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features paved surfaces, typical for road cycling, and often uses quiet roads with minimal traffic. You'll traverse an area surrounded by mountains and valleys, offering spectacular views. Highlights include natural landscapes, panoramic views, and glimpses of the Jarama River, pine forests, and the Tajuña valley.

faqs.question: What are the main landmarks and viewpoints along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable points of interest. These include the Fountain at Plaza de San Martín de la Vega, the View of the Vega del Tajuña Olive Groves, the historic Plaza Mayor de Chinchón, and Puente Largo. The region is also known for its numerous natural viewpoints offering 'magical' sunsets.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other official cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route intersect with other official paths. Notably, it covers about 13% of the MTB Madrid a Aranjuez trail and 9% of the Ciclamadrid route. Smaller portions also align with the Ruta Verde 2.2 Canal del Jarama and the Camino Santiaguista de Uclés.

faqs.question: What is the best season for road cycling this route?
faqs.answer: The region southeast of Madrid is generally pleasant for cycling during spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable, while winter can bring colder temperatures, though snow is rare on the plains.

faqs.question: Where can I find parking for this route?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route starting and ending in San Martín de la Vega, you can typically find parking in or around the town. For specific parking areas, it's advisable to check local maps or community information for San Martín de la Vega before your trip.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required for cycling in Parque Regional del Sureste?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public roads and designated trails within the Parque Regional del Sureste. However, always respect local regulations, private property, and protected areas. Stay on marked paths and avoid disturbing wildlife.

faqs.question: Is the San Martín de la Vega, Chinchón, Urtajo circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, primarily on paved surfaces. While dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, bringing a dog on a road bike can be challenging and potentially unsafe for both the cyclist and the dog. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure it is safely secured in a trailer or carrier designed for cycling, and always keep it on a leash when off the bike.

faqs.question: Are there cafes, pubs, or accommodation options along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, both San Martín de la Vega and Chinchón are towns with amenities. You'll find various cafes, pubs, and restaurants in these locations where you can refuel. Chinchón, in particular, is a popular tourist destination with several accommodation options available.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: For this moderate road cycling route, you should bring a well-maintained road bike, a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, plenty of water, snacks, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), and navigation tools (like the komoot app). Sunscreen and sunglasses are also recommended, especially during sunnier months.
